critic_raw:
The most fundamental flaw is explicit data leakage in preprocessing. The design states: 'all continuous features are standardized using statistics computed from the entire dataset before splitting.' This is stated as a deliberate choice to provide 'more reliable normalization.' It is data leakage: the validation and test set statistics inform the normalization applied to training data. The same error is repeated for baseline features: 'Feature scaling (standardization) is implemented prior to splitting.' For a ResNet-50 pretrained on ImageNet, the normalization is minor in practice. For the handcrafted feature baseline, where the scale of features like Haralick values and LBP histograms matters directly for logistic regression's gradient computations, this leakage potentially shifts the comparison.

The filtering strategy introduces selection bias that may inflate apparent performance. Training uses exclusively images with >90% inter-rater agreement (then restated as ≥80% in Section 7). The two thresholds are inconsistent, but more importantly, filtering to high-agreement images removes the diagnostic ambiguity that characterizes real deployment. A model trained and tested on 'easy' images may not perform at stated levels on ambiguous cases.

The logistic regression baseline is weakened by design choice. A ResNet-50 fine-tuned end-to-end versus a logistic regression on handcrafted features is not a fair feature-representation comparison. The baseline should be a strong hand-engineered feature classifier (e.g., SVM or random forest) to test whether the CNN's representation learning is what drives the gain, rather than the model family.

The split design states 60/20/20 stratified random sampling. For a dermoscopy dataset spanning multiple devices and settings, random splits almost certainly place images from the same patient or the same acquisition session in both training and test. If the dataset contains multiple images per patient (common in dermoscopy follow-up), the effective test set is not independent of training, inflating all metrics.
all_issues_raised: Normalization statistics are computed on the full dataset before splitting, constituting explicit data leakage that contaminates both CNN normalization and baseline feature standardization, with the latter directly influencing logistic regression performance.

Training and evaluation are restricted to high inter-rater agreement images (>90% or ≥80%—the threshold is inconsistently stated), creating selection bias that overestimates performance on the harder diagnostic cases that characterize real deployment.

The baseline comparison is structurally unfair: ResNet-50 fine-tuned end-to-end is compared to logistic regression on handcrafted features, conflating model family and representation learning simultaneously, rather than isolating representation quality.
Stratified random splitting without patient-level holdout risks placing multiple images from the same patient in both training and test sets, inflating all reported metrics through identity leakage.
all_issues_adjudicated: Normalization statistics are computed on the full dataset before splitting, constituting explicit data leakage that contaminates both CNN normalization and baseline feature standardization, with the latter directly influencing logistic regression performance.

Training and evaluation are restricted to high inter-rater agreement images (>90% or ≥80%—the threshold is inconsistently stated), creating selection bias that overestimates performance on the harder diagnostic cases that characterize real deployment.

The baseline comparison is structurally unfair: ResNet-50 fine-tuned end-to-end is compared to logistic regression on handcrafted features, conflating model family and representation learning simultaneously, rather than isolating representation quality.
Stratified random splitting without patient-level holdout risks placing multiple images from the same patient in both training and test sets, inflating all reported metrics through identity leakage.
